column_name.description:
Output analytical column name. Must not be one of the built-in output
columns: `tenant_id`, `table_name`, or `__id`.

AnalyticsManifest.description:
Top-level analytics manifest that registers source stream tables as
analytical tables.

tables.description: Table registrations to create and maintain in the analytical catalog.

version.description: Manifest format version supported by this crate.

CheckpointHealth.description: Source checkpoint position for a single source table shard.

position.description: Opaque source stream position to resume from.

source_table_name.description: Source table name from the analytics source configuration.

updated_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when this checkpoint was updated.

order.description: Sort direction. Defaults to ascending when omitted.

oneOf.description: Sort direction. Defaults to ascending when omitted.

DiagnosticsResponse.description: Process diagnostics including source poller state.

ErrorResponse.description: Error response for invalid requests and query failures.

example.error: query must be a single read-only SELECT statement

error.example: query must be a single read-only SELECT statement

outcome.description: Final ingestion action performed by the engine.

oneOf.description: Domain-agnostic analytics wire format.
Standard stream event envelope containing a DynamoDB-compatible record.

record.description:
Stream record in the analytics wire format or a standard stream event
envelope.

record_key.description:
Stable row key for idempotent writes. Defaults to the stream sequence or
event id when omitted.

oneOf.description: Hash bucket a column into a fixed number of buckets.
Time bucket a timestamp column.
Optional partition bucketing transform.

PrimitiveColumnType.description: Primitive `DuckDB` column type supported by the manifest contract.

ProjectionColumn.description: Typed projected column sourced from a nested source attribute path.

column_name.description:
Output analytical column name. Must not be one of the built-in output
columns: `tenant_id`, `table_name`, or `__id`.

column_type.description: Optional output column type. Defaults to VARCHAR when omitted.

QueryExpression.description: Structured expression used by filters, grouping, and ordering.

oneOf.description: Registered analytical column expression.
JSON document path expression.

path.description: Dot-separated JSON path relative to the document root.

direction.description: Sort direction. Defaults to ascending when omitted.

oneOf.description: Sort direction. Defaults to ascending when omitted.

QueryPredicate.description: Structured predicate used in WHERE clauses.

oneOf.description: Equality predicate.
Inequality predicate.
Greater-than predicate.
Greater-than-or-equal predicate.
Less-than predicate.
Less-than-or-equal predicate.
IS NULL predicate.
IS NOT NULL predicate.

QueryResponse.description: Query result rows as JSON objects.

oneOf.description: Select a registered analytical column.

alias.description: Optional output alias. Defaults to the column name when omitted.

oneOf.description: Select a path from the configured JSON document column.

alias.description: Required output alias for the extracted value.

path.description: Dot-separated JSON path relative to the document root.

oneOf.description: Select count(*).

alias.description: Required output alias for the count column.

RetentionPolicy.description: Static retention policy for one analytical table.

timestamp.description: Timestamp used as the start time for retention.

RetentionTimestamp.description: Timestamp basis used to compute row expiry.

oneOf.description: Read a unix epoch millisecond value from a source item attribute path.
Use the aux-analytics ingestion time.
Use the record key supplied to the ingest endpoint.
Hash the source stream key map.
Hash a string attribute value from the source item.

attribute_name.description: Source string attribute containing row identity.

oneOf.description:
Extract row identity from a named regex capture group on a string
attribute.

capture.description: Named capture group to use as row identity.

regex.description: Regular expression with the configured capture group.

SortOrder.description: Sort direction for clustering and structured order clauses.

SourceHealth.description: Current source polling and checkpoint health for diagnostics responses.

checkpoints.description: Last known checkpoint positions by source table and shard.

last_error.description: Last source polling error message, when present.
last_error.example: analytics source http request failed with status 503: unavailable

last_error_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds for the most recent poll error.

last_poll_started_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the most recent poll started.

last_success_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds for the most recent successful poll.

poller_enabled.description: Whether source polling is enabled for this process.

table_count.description: Number of source tables configured for polling.

total_ingest_errors.description: Total source records that failed ingestion.

total_poll_errors.description: Total poll cycles that failed since process start.

total_polls.description: Total poll cycles attempted since process start.

total_records_ingested.description: Total records successfully ingested by the source poller.

SourceHealthStatus.description: Health state for the optional source poller.

eventID.description: Source event id used as a fallback ingestion row key.

eventName.description: Source event operation name, such as INSERT, MODIFY, or REMOVE.

Keys.description: Primary key attributes for the source record.

NewImage.description: New record image for insert and update events.

OldImage.description: Previous record image for update and delete events.

StorageStreamRecord.description:
Domain-agnostic storage stream record accepted by the analytics ingestion
API.

Keys.description: Source storage key attributes for the record.

NewImage.description: New source image for insert and update events.

OldImage.description: Previous source image for update and delete events.

SequenceNumber.description:
Source stream sequence number or equivalent monotonically ordered
position.

StructuredQuery.description: Structured query tree compiled to safe SQL by the analytics engine.

analytics_table_name.description: Registered analytical table name to query.

filters.description: Optional filter predicates joined with AND.

select.description: Select list. At least one select item is required.

TableRegistration.description:
Registration for one analytical table populated from one source table or
source table family.

analytics_table_name.description: DuckDB-compatible analytical table name to create and query.

columns.description:
Explicit analytical columns. When present, these define table shape
directly. Custom column names must not use the built-in output
column names `tenant_id`, `table_name`, or `__id`.

condition_expression.description:
Optional DynamoDB-compatible condition expression that filters source
items before ingestion.

document_column.description:
JSON document column containing the full source item. Set null to omit
the full document. Must not use the built-in output column names
`tenant_id`, `table_name`, or `__id`.

expression_attribute_names.description:
Expression attribute name aliases referenced by condition or projection
expressions.

expression_attribute_values.description:
Expression attribute values referenced by condition or projection
expressions.

projection_attribute_names.description:
Simple top-level source attribute names to project as VARCHAR columns.
Values must not use the built-in output column names `tenant_id`,
`table_name`, or `__id`.

projection_columns.description:
Typed projection columns from nested source attributes.
Projection output names must not use the built-in output column names
`tenant_id`, `table_name`, or `__id`.

oneOf.description: Optional static retention policy for rows in this analytical table.

row_identity.description: Rule used to derive the stable row id for upserts and deletes.

oneOf.description: Rule used to derive the stable row id for upserts and deletes.

skip_delete.description:
Whether delete events should be ignored instead of removing analytical
rows.

source_table_name.description: Source storage table name to read from.

source_table_name_prefix.description:
Optional source table prefix for families of source tables that share
one registration.

tenant_id.description:
Static tenant id to write into ingested rows when the source does not
carry tenant identity.

tenant_selector.description: Rule used to derive tenant id from records when tenant_id is not static.

oneOf.description:
Rule used to derive tenant id from records when `tenant_id` is not
static.

TenantQueryRequest.description:
Tenant-scoped structured query request for callers that should not
construct SQL.

query.description: Structured query tree compiled by the analytics engine.

target_tenant_id.description:
Tenant id that the query is authorized to read. The API injects this as
an engine-owned tenant predicate; callers must not rely on their own
tenant filters for isolation.

oneOf.description: Do not write tenant ids.
Use the source table name as tenant context.
Read tenant id from a string attribute.

attribute_name.description: Source string attribute containing tenant id.

oneOf.description:
Extract tenant id from a named regex capture group on a string
attribute.

capture.description: Named capture group to use as tenant id.

regex.description: Regular expression with the configured capture group.

oneOf.description:
Prefer table name when available, otherwise parse a partition-key
prefix.
Parse tenant id from the prefix of a partition key attribute.

UnscopedSqlQueryRequest.description:
Explicitly unscoped raw SQL query request. The HTTP API validates this shape
but rejects execution until an admin authorization boundary exists.

example.sql: select email, org_id from users where org_id = 'org-a' order by email

sql.description:
Raw SQL text. The HTTP API rejects raw SQL execution because arbitrary
SQL cannot prove tenant isolation without a separate admin boundary.
sql.example: select email, org_id from users where org_id = 'org-a' order by email

query.description:
Structured query tree compiled by the analytics engine without a tenant
predicate. Use only for explicitly unscoped data.

200.description: Service diagnostics including source polling and checkpoint health
Query tenant-scoped rows as JSON objects

400.description: Raw SQL is not available through HTTP without an admin boundary

200.description: Query explicitly unscoped rows as JSON objects

tags.description: Analytics manifest, ingestion, and query API
tags.name: Analytics
